forward_src:
def forward(self, **inputs):
    import re

    input_name = inputs.get("input_name", "") or ""
    input_address = inputs.get("input_address", "") or ""
    match_name = inputs.get("match_name", "") or ""
    match_address = inputs.get("match_address", "") or ""
    try:
        distance = float(inputs.get("distance", 0.0) or 0.0)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        distance = 0.0

    # NOTE: distance units appear to be in meters in the examples
    # (e.g. 5.77 meters for same-address pair, 278 for nearby different places,
    # 8.8 for very close different places). We'll treat distance in meters.

    def normalize(text):
        text = text.lower().strip()
        # Strip trailing store numbers like #72, # 72
        text = re.sub(r"#\s*\d+", " ", text)
        noise = [
            r"\b(inc|incorporated|llc|l\.l\.c\.|ltd|limited|co|corp|corporation|company)\b",
            r"\b(the|and|&)\b",
            r"\b(restaurant|cafe|bar|grill|shop|store|bakeshop|market|supermarket)\b",
        ]
        for pat in noise:
            text = re.sub(pat, " ", text)
        text = re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", " ", text)
        text = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text).strip()
        return text

    def core_name(text):
        # Aggressive normalization: also strip digits
        n = normalize(text)
        n = re.sub(r"\b\d+\b", " ", n)
        n = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", n).strip()
        return n

    def tokens(text):
        return set(t for t in text.split() if t)

    def jaccard(a, b):
        if not a or not b:
            return 0.0
        return len(a & b) / len(a | b)

    def normalize_address(addr):
        addr = addr.lower().strip()
        repl = {
            r"\bstreet\b": "st",
            r"\bavenue\b": "ave",
            r"\bboulevard\b": "blvd",
            r"\broad\b": "rd",
            r"\bdrive\b": "dr",
            r"\blane\b": "ln",
            r"\bcourt\b": "ct",
            r"\bplace\b": "pl",
            r"\bsuite\b": "ste",
            r"\bnorth\b": "n",
            r"\bsouth\b": "s",
            r"\beast\b": "e",
            r"\bwest\b": "w",
        }
        for pat, sub in repl.items():
            addr = re.sub(pat, sub, addr)
        addr = re.sub(r"[^\w\s]", " ", addr)
        addr = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", addr).strip()
        return addr

    def extract_street_number(addr):
        m = re.match(r"^\s*(\d+)", addr)
        return m.group(1) if m else None

    def address_core(addr):
        # Drop unit/suite indicators at the end like "I", "A", "ste 100", etc.
        a = normalize_address(addr)
        # Remove trailing single-letter unit indicators
        a = re.sub(r"\s+[a-z]$", "", a)
        # Remove "ste <num>" or "unit <num>" or "apt <num>"
        a = re.sub(r"\b(ste|unit|apt)\s+\w+", " ", a)
        a = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", a).strip()
        return a

    name_a_norm = normalize(input_name)
    name_b_norm = normalize(match_name)
    core_a = core_name(input_name)
    core_b = core_name(match_name)

    addr_a_norm = address_core(input_address)
    addr_b_norm = address_core(match_address)

    name_tokens_a = tokens(name_a_norm)
    name_tokens_b = tokens(name_b_norm)
    name_sim = jaccard(name_tokens_a, name_tokens_b)
    name_exact = name_a_norm == name_b_norm and bool(name_a_norm)

    core_tokens_a = tokens(core_a)
    core_tokens_b = tokens(core_b)
    core_sim = jaccard(core_tokens_a, core_tokens_b)
    core_exact = core_a == core_b and bool(core_a)
    # Subset check: one core name's tokens are a subset of the other
    core_subset = (
        bool(core_tokens_a) and bool(core_tokens_b) and
        (core_tokens_a.issubset(core_tokens_b) or core_tokens_b.issubset(core_tokens_a))
    )

    addr_tokens_a = tokens(addr_a_norm)
    addr_tokens_b = tokens(addr_b_norm)
    addr_sim = jaccard(addr_tokens_a, addr_tokens_b)
    addr_exact = addr_a_norm == addr_b_norm and bool(addr_a_norm)

    num_a = extract_street_number(addr_a_norm)
    num_b = extract_street_number(addr_b_norm)
    street_num_match = (num_a is not None and num_b is not None and num_a == num_b)
    street_num_conflict = (num_a is not None and num_b is not None and num_a != num_b)

    # Distance thresholds in meters
    VERY_CLOSE = 20.0   # ~same building
    CLOSE = 50.0
    NEAR = 150.0
    FAR = 500.0

    # Deterministic decisions:

    # Same address (including same street number) + same/subset core name => same
    if addr_exact and (core_exact or core_subset or core_sim >= 0.5):
        return dspy.Prediction(is_same=True)

    # Very close + matching street number + name is exact or one is subset of other
    if distance <= VERY_CLOSE and street_num_match and (core_exact or core_subset):
        return dspy.Prediction(is_same=True)

    # Very close + matching street number + very high name similarity
    if distance <= CLOSE and street_num_match and core_sim >= 0.7:
        return dspy.Prediction(is_same=True)

    # Far apart => different
    if distance > FAR:
        return dspy.Prediction(is_same=False)

    # Conflicting street numbers and not extremely close => different
    if street_num_conflict and distance > VERY_CLOSE:
        return dspy.Prediction(is_same=False)

    # Very low name similarity and different addresses and not co-located => different
    if core_sim < 0.2 and not addr_exact and distance > VERY_CLOSE:
        return dspy.Prediction(is_same=False)

    # Ambiguous: ask the LM.
    judged = self.judge(
        input_name=input_name,
        input_address=input_address,
        match_name=match_name,
        match_address=match_address,
        distance=distance,
    )
    return dspy.Prediction(is_same=bool(judged.is_same))
